mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling period, or forward returns are negative in >50% of co-occurring months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of expansion-phase observations during a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger months over a 3-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return (p > 0.05) over a 4-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of positive GDP growth quarters across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production combined with rising core inflation indicates stagflationary pressures, forcing equity markets to price in lower growth and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling validation period.
